paper_title: Landscape and Sediment Analysis in Southern Italy

methods.description: This method involves the collection and analysis of sediment samples from various sites in southern Italy to understand the historical landscape and vegetation. The samples are used to provide evidence of local vegetation and to characterize local sediments for comparison with extra-local sediments.

steps.description: Survey the countryside surrounding San Felice for suitable exposures and springs to sample for pollen and macrofossils.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and locate sampling sites for sediment collection

steps.description: Collect over 95 sediment samples from two exposures and trench and take five cores from one of the spring mounds.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples and core samples from identified sites

steps.description: Submit three samples for radiocarbon dating at Beta Analytic, Inc.
Process 24 sediment samples for grain size parameters using a Beckman Coulter Inc. Multisizer 3 Coulter Counter and a Malvern Mastersizer 2000 laser particle analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment samples for grain size

steps.description: In summer 2013, sample additional sites identified in western Puglia and central and eastern Basilicata provinces.
steps.searchable_summary: collect additional sediment samples from new sites

steps.description: Send sediment samples to USDA Permitted lab at the University of Nevada, Reno for analysis.
Send radiocarbon samples to CEDAD -AMS Radiocarbon Dating and IBA Facility at the University of Lecce.

methods.description: This model explores the interactions between climate, vegetation, geomorphology, and human activity to understand landscape transformation and erosion rates in southern Italy.

steps.description: Modify the Langbein and Schumm diagram to fit the southern Italian context, showing the relationship between sediment yield, effective precipitation, and vegetation cover.
steps.searchable_summary: adapt existing geomorphological model to local context

steps.description: Suggest erosion rates under different precipitation regimes and vegetation covers, considering human impact.
steps.searchable_summary: model erosion rates based on climate and human activity
